LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SONG LEE'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 28, 2008. THE PARTIES ARE TO SUBMIT A BRIEF, NOT TO EXCEED 10 PAGES, AS TO WHAT IS REQUIRED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT AN ACTION IS "FRIVOLOUS, UNREASONABLE OR WITHOUT FOUNDATION," AND WHAT FACTS SUPPORT OR DO NOT SUPPORT DEFENDANT'S CONTENTION THAT PLAINTIFF LEARNED THAT HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 19955 CLAIM WAS FRIVOLOUS. THE SUBMISSION IS TO BE FILED ON OR BEFORE AUGUST 25, 2008. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SONG LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANT SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO D.R.E.E.S. BASED ON A LACK OF STANDING. MOLSKI V MANDARIN TORCH RESTAURANT (2005) 359 F. SUPP 924, 935. COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S LATE FILED OPPOSITION. COURT TAKES THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION UNDER SUBMISSION. COURT STATES ITS RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ON THE RECORD. AN ORDER IS SIGNED ON THE RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS IN OPEN COURT. A COPY IS SERVED UPON DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL LEFT BEFORE BEING SERVED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL AGREED TO SERVE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITH THE ORDER ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, SUBMITTED ON MAY-23-2008 BY PATRICK J. MAHONEY.

The parties are to submit a brief, not to exceed 10 pages, as to what is required to demonstrate that an action is "frivolous, unreasonable or without foundation," and what facts support or do not support defendant's contention that plaintiff learned that Health & Safety Code section 19955 claim was frivolous.
